SWIPE / REVEAL — FROZEN MODEL (PLAN-swipe-reveal.md stage 1)
GENERATED, DO NOT EDIT.  Regenerate: node tools/gen-swipe-model.mjs
Guarded by: test/swipe-model.test.js

Every row below is one of:
  [parity] reproduces what js/app.js does today, verified at the named region
  [policy] NEW behaviour the rewrite must CLOSE — today this path is UNDEFINED

SOURCE FINGERPRINTS — if one changes, the mirrored rule must be RE-VERIFIED
before this document is trusted again. Never update a pinned constant alone.
  transition branches   55f4d0411e8e301f
  navTo stack rule      0e84abdf6d072586
  begin/nav-relation    ac356cd1a669c2a3
  end/state-routing     9a82592f5d21db7b
  begin/supersession    c70d4ed49257af8e

1. REGISTRY — PARTLY DERIVED, PARTLY PINNED (say which, and mean it)
   12 screens, 132 ordered name pairs.
   DERIVED  the settings sub-screens, read from Nav.SETTINGS_SUBS.
   PINNED   home, books, authors, authorBooks, files, options, nowplaying are
            hand-listed in the generator. A future browse-family or overlay screen
            would NOT appear here on its own — so the screen-name census below is
            what makes its absence loud. This is a pin, not a derivation, and an
            external review of .218 was right that the earlier wording claimed
            more than the implementation delivered. The proper fix is one screen
            registry exported by Nav; that is a PRODUCTION change, and stages 1-2
            touch no production code.
   census (every screen name in a production descriptor literal or nav control):
     authorBooks authors books downloads files home nowplaying options
   excluded: app — history.replaceState({ v: 'app' }) — a history state, not a screen descriptor
   (A settings sub-screen may still SHOW UP in the census when some other literal
    or nav control names it — `downloads` does, via the book-menu control. But the
    census does not RELY on that: every sub is derived from SETTINGS_SUBS, so one
    that appears in no literal is still in the registry. openSub pushes { v } from
    a VARIABLE, which is why the subs cannot be counted on to appear here.)
   The structural matrix lives in docs/transition-matrix.generated.txt and is
   imported here rather than restated — one derivation, one home.

2. DESCRIPTOR SCENARIOS (§4.3) — names are NOT enough
   navTo REPLACES the stack top when `v` matches AND the descriptor carries
   neither `author` nor `book`; otherwise it PUSHES. The guard tests PRESENCE,
   not identity, so authorBooks(A) over authorBooks(A) pushes just like (B).
   Consequence, derived rather than asserted: a pair whose forward navigation
   REPLACES can never occupy two adjacent stack slots, so it is UNREACHABLE as a
   back-swipe. That is why 132 name pairs cannot express this space.

   ⚠️ That derivation is only sound because of the append census below. navTo is
   NOT the only site that appends to navStack. The invariant that actually holds:
   EVERY append path either applies navTo's replace guard, or appends a descriptor
   whose `v` differs from the current top. A new append site would break this
   silently, so the sites are pinned:
     js/app.js:127   let navStack = [{ v: 'home' }];
     js/app.js:141   if (cur && cur.v === desc.v && !desc.author && !desc.book) navStack[navStack.length - 1] = desc;
     js/app.js:142   else navStack.push(desc);
     js/app.js:165   navStack.push({ v }); fwdStack.length = 0;
     js/app.js:850   else if (cur.newNav) { navStack.push(cur.dest); fwdStack.length = 0; }   // NP → chapters is a fresh forward nav
     js/app.js:851   else navStack.push(fwdStack.pop());
     js/app.js:1376  navStack = [{ v: 'home' }];

   case                              from -> to                              result
   --------------------------------  --------------------------------------  ------------------------
   different type                    books() -> authors()                    pane=yes abort=rerender back=reachable
   same type, different identity     authorBooks(A) -> authorBooks(B)        pane=yes abort=rerender back=reachable
   same type, different identity     files(A) -> files(B)                    pane=yes abort=rerender back=reachable
   same semantic descriptor          books() -> books()                      pane=yes abort=rerender back=UNREACHABLE
   same parameterized identity       authorBooks(A) -> authorBooks(A)        pane=yes abort=rerender back=reachable
   browse -> home                    books() -> home()                       pane=yes abort=none     back=reachable
   home -> browse                    home() -> books()                       pane=yes abort=none     back=reachable
   overlay -> overlay                general() -> options()                  pane=no  abort=none     back=reachable
   overlay -> browse                 options() -> books()                    pane=no  abort=none     back=reachable
   browse -> overlay                 books() -> nowplaying()                 pane=no  abort=none     back=reachable
   nowplaying -> files (new-forward)  nowplaying() -> files(A)                pane=no  abort=none     back=reachable
   malformed payload                 authorBooks(—) -> books()               REJECTED source malformed-descriptor:authorBooks-without-author
   malformed payload                 books() -> files(—)                     REJECTED destination malformed-descriptor:files-without-book
   not a transition                  home() -> home()                        REJECTED not-a-transition:home-to-home

   Two independently allocated but semantically equal descriptors classify
   identically — the model keys on VALUE, never on object identity.

3. THE FOUR RESOLVED RULES (§2.6)
   [parity] NP pill
        cloned when nowplaying is EITHER endpoint; a third mover with the
        same lifetime as outgoing/incoming; from NP -> base 0 and body loses
        np-locked; to NP -> base off
        verified at: start(): pill built in both the fromV and toV nowplaying branches
   [parity] navigation relation
        back = left edge, dest navStack[len-2]; new-forward = right edge
        FROM nowplaying only -> filesDescForCurrent(), newNav, commit PUSHES
        and CLEARS fwdStack; forward-replay = right edge otherwise, dest
        fwdStack top; a back commit pushes the popped entry onto fwdStack
        verified at: begin(): the fromLeft / from.v===nowplaying / fwdStack.length branch chain
   [parity] overlay->overlay reachability
        ESTABLISHED, not deferred: openSub() pushes a sub-screen ON TOP of
        Options, so a left-edge swipe yields e.g. general->options directly.
        Button navigation between overlays uses Nav.overlayFilmstrip(), a
        DIFFERENT path this plan does not touch and must not assume shares
        code
        verified at: openSub(): navStack.push({v}) while currentDesc().v === options
   [parity] scroll policy
        FROZEN FOR PARITY. Home entry uses window.scrollTo(0,1) when
        resetScroll is set. This is NOT an iOS-26 runway requirement —
        nav.js says the navbar seater is body.home-tall and the 1px is a
        remnant of the abandoned runway theory. Stage 1 does NOT decide
        whether the 1px survives; remove it AFTER device parity in a
        separate commit
        verified at: nav.js: body.home-tall seating comment; plan §2.6.4 / T12

   scroll policy object (plan states policy; Browse resolves coordinates):
        onCommit   destination-entry
        onAbort    restore-session-start
        overlay    preserve-document
        reveal     destination-final

4. GESTURE-ENDING INPUTS, BY STATE (§3.7, I19)
   state              basis     outcome
   -----------------  --------  -------------------------------------------------
   ARMED              [parity]  finish, NO navigation, release listeners, remain on source
                              at: end(): releaseGesture() then returns at `if (!cur.live)`
   DRAGGING           [parity]  ordinary travel+velocity decision via settleSession(); touchcancel CAN commit
                              at: touchcancel binds the SAME onEnd as touchend
   SETTLING or later  [parity]  ignore as a stale duplicate event
                              at: end() returns at `if (!d)` — d is nulled before settle()

5. SUPERSESSION — a new touch during ARMED or DRAGGING (§3.7, I20)
   [parity] PARITY IS SUPERSEDE, NOT REJECT. begin() rejects only while
            `finishing` is true; otherwise it hard-resets and arms the new
            gesture. I17(a) is the separate rule: while an active session is
            SETTLING / FINALIZING / REVEALING a new gesture does NOT arm, and
            that session's pane is NOT disposed to make room (I10 must hold).
   [parity] What today ACTUALLY does on supersession: releaseGesture, dropRowHold,
            d = null, resetSwipeStyles, applyScreen(currentDesc(), {render:false}).
            Listeners, row hold, panes and inline styles are all released; the nav
            stack and navbar return to the source. Movers are torn down BY
            OWNERSHIP (§3.2) — panes disposed, decorations removed, borrowed real
            views restored and NEVER removed.

   ⚠️ TWO SEPARATE DEFECTS live here, and BOTH are new policy. An earlier draft of
   this document labelled the whole of supersession [parity]; that was wrong, and
   an external review of .218 caught it. The pre-stack recovery row this section
   leans on is itself [policy], so "recovered pre-stack" cannot be parity.

   [policy] (1) SCROLL. Today's hard reset does NOT restore the starting scroll,
            so a superseded browse->browse drag can be left at the DESTINATION's
            scroll (its mid-drag render ran positionOnEnter).
   [policy] (2) SOURCE CONTENT. `render:false` means nothing re-renders the source
            into the shared #browse, so the host keeps the DESTINATION's content
            while the stack and navbar say source. MEASURED at .218:
            renders = ["books","authors","books"] after Authors->Books is
            superseded. That is an I11 violation and the same wrong-page/wrong-tap
            class as .178 — the nav says one screen and Browse shows another.
            Both are covered by `{todo}` tests in test/swipe-invariants.test.js.

6. TERMINATION REASONS (§3.7)
   reason                      basis     nav              screen        scroll              pane
   --------------------------  --------  ---------------  ------------  ------------------  --------------
   vertical-intent (pre-drag)  [parity]  unchanged        source        unchanged           none built
   touch-cancel (dragging)     [parity]  settle decision  from decision  commit/abort        normal settle
   hard-reset (leftover)       [parity]  unchanged        currentDesc()  NOT restored today  dispose orphan

7. RECOVERY — ONE RULE, KEYED ON PHASE (§3.7, I18)
   reasons: lease-invalid | destination-gone | finalize-threw | superseded
   There are NO per-reason screen/scroll rules. After the stack has been mutated
   the stack is authoritative, for every reason alike.
   phase        basis     navigation           screen                    scroll
   -----------  --------  -------------------  ------------------------  ----------------------
   pre-stack    [policy]  unchanged            restore source            restore session start
   post-stack   [policy]  stack authoritative  render current stack top  destination policy
   [policy] ALL RECOVERY ROWS ARE NEW. Today finalization has a try/finally for
            the row hold and nothing else. These are gaps the rewrite must CLOSE,
            not behaviour it must reproduce.

8. PANE DISPOSAL (§3.4) — release is the normal path, dispose is the emergency
   permitted dispose reasons: superseded, lease-invalid, finalize-threw, hard-reset, destination-gone
   pane.release()       ONLY from reveal(), ONLY after under-view readiness AND
                        the paint barrier (I10).
   pane.dispose(reason) idempotent emergency teardown; bypasses I10 deliberately.

9. AUTHORITATIVE SETTLED DESCRIPTOR (I11) — selected by OUTCOME, not by stack
   commit              -> the committed destination
   abort               -> the original source
   pre-stack recovery  -> the original source
   post-stack recovery -> the current stack top
   hard reset          -> currentDesc()
   Draft 5 said "destination descriptor", which is IMPOSSIBLE on an abort: the
   destination is precisely the thing NOT navigated to.

10. PARITY vs NEW POLICY LEDGER (§8A) — the short answer
    NEW POLICY, and only these (derived from NEW_POLICIES, asserted exactly by
    the gate so none can be silently dropped):
      - the recovery table (§7), pre/post-stack, all reasons
      - restoring the starting scroll when a gesture is SUPERSEDED
      - re-rendering the SOURCE into #browse when a gesture is
      SUPERSEDED (added after review of .218 found the first draft had
      labelled it [parity])
    Everything else in this document is preserved deliberately, including the
    1px Home entry scroll and the overlay->browse hidden-host side effect
    (abort leaves #browse holding the destination's content; canonicalizing it
    is a SEPARATE cleanup after parity).
